title: Your First AI Project: Scratch to Python

modules.title: Teaching a Computer to Recognize Patterns
modules.sub: Discover how machines spot patterns the way you do
modules.description: You step into the science fair story and face your first real question: how does a computer actually learn to tell things apart? You explore everyday examples of pattern recognition and map out what your AI project needs to do before a single line of code is written.

modules.title: Build a Classifier in Scratch: No Guessing
modules.sub: Train a working classifier using blocks, not luck
modules.description: Using Scratch, you collect training data, label it, and build a model that can sort inputs into categories. Every decision — what data to use, how much to collect, when the model is good enough — is yours to make, and the story shows you exactly what goes right or wrong as a result.

modules.title: Cross the Bridge: Your First Python AI Script
modules.sub: Translate your Scratch logic into real Python code
modules.description: You take the classifier you already understand and rebuild its core logic in Python, line by line. The module breaks down what each piece of code is actually doing so you're never just copying — you're explaining it to yourself as you go.

modules.title: Ship It: Present Your Working Project
modules.sub: Show your model, explain your choices, own your work
modules.description: You prepare your finished project for the science fair audience inside the story, deciding how to demonstrate your model and describe what it does in plain language. The module pushes you to reflect on what your AI can do, what it can't, and why those limits matter.

synopsis: You're building an AI for a school science fair — except this story follows you through every wrong turn, broken model, and breakthrough until you have something real to show. You make the build decisions; the story shows you what happens.
rationale: Most coding courses for this age stop at syntax and never let students experience the specific loop of training, testing, and improving that defines actual AI development.
learning_outcome: Students can build and train a simple pattern-recognition model in Scratch, then transfer that same logic into a beginner Python script and explain what the code is actually doing.
source_signals: Show HN: PickCode – An educational coding environment for students after Scratch
Learning about artificial intelligence: A hub of MIT resources for K-12 students - MIT Media Lab
nearest_existing: Code Is Now the Easiest Part
